translations.en: Daros
translations.zh: 達羅斯

translations.en: a skinny, awkward looking man, wearing boots that come up past his knees.
translations.zh: 一個瘦削、模樣滑稽、靴子高過膝蓋的男人。

translations.en: "Hey hey hey! Welcome to the finest sewers in Britannia.
translations.zh: 「嘿嘿嘿！歡迎光臨不列顛尼亞最棒的下水道。

translations.en: "You look like you need a nickname, so I'll call you 'ducks.'
translations.zh: 「你這副模樣好像需要個外號，我就叫你「鴨子」吧。
translations.note: 後半（或獨立段落），未閉合

translations.en: "My name's Daros, old duck." He shakes your hand.
translations.zh: 「我叫達羅斯，老鴨子。」他和你握手。

translations.en: "Well, ducky, I keep the @sewers running the way they should."
translations.zh: 「嗯，小鴨子，我負責維持@sewers下水道正常運作。」

translations.en: "When work is slow, sometimes I play a @joke or two on my @friends to keep things lively." He winks at you.
translations.zh: 「工作不忙的時候，我有時會對@friends朋友們開個@joke玩笑，讓氣氛活躍一點。」他向你眨了眨眼。

translations.en: "Even though we don't know each other that well, I'd like to think of you as my friend already."
translations.zh: 「雖然我們還不是很熟，但我已經想把你當成我的朋友了。」

translations.en: "Oh you know, ducks, the usual kinds." He nudges you in the ribs.
translations.zh: 「哦，你懂的，鴨子，就是那些常見的玩笑。」他用手肘頂了頂你的肋骨。

translations.en: "@Britain is the biggest city in the world, and all those people use a lot of @water."
translations.zh: 「@Britain不列顛是世上最大的城市，那麼多人用了大量的@water水。」

translations.en: "All the runoff drains down here. It's not a bad place to work, really, except for the @rats."
translations.zh: 「所有廢水都排到這裡來。說真的，這地方還不錯，就是@rats老鼠多了點。」

translations.en: "They're pretty big, ducky, but not to worry."
translations.zh: 「那些老鼠挺大的，小鴨子，但別擔心。」

translations.en: "They usually won't eat you until after they've killed you first."
translations.zh: 「它們通常要先把你殺死，才會吃你。」

translations.en: "Of course, if you have a set of @panpipes, I hear you can just pipe them away..."
translations.zh: 「當然，如果你有一套@panpipes排笛，聽說用笛聲就能把它們引走……」

translations.en: "I don't have a set myself. I usually just @run away."
translations.zh: 「我自己沒有。通常我就直接@run跑就是了。」

translations.en: "It's real good exercise!"
translations.zh: 「是很好的運動！」

translations.en: "I wouldn't go swimming around here, ducks."
translations.zh: 「我勸你別在這附近游泳，鴨子。」

translations.en: "Go back up and have a look! I'm sure it's still there."
translations.zh: 「上去看看吧！我相信它還在那裡。」

translations.en: He grins, obviously delighted with his own sense of humor.
translations.zh: 他咧嘴笑著，顯然對自己的幽默感十分滿意。

translations.en: "See you around, old duck."
translations.zh: 「回頭見，老鴨子。」

translations.en: *He gives you a hearty slap on the back as you leave, which almost knocks one of the straps on your pack loose.
translations.zh: *你離開時，他用力拍了你一下後背，差點把你背包的一根帶子拍鬆了。

translations.en: "That's not something I talk about much, down here..."
translations.zh: 「那不是我在這下面常談的話題……」

translator_notes: 達羅斯是不列顛城下水道工人，愛說笑、熱情，把所有人叫做「鴨子/老鴨子」（ducks/old duck）。「排笛趕老鼠」暗示玩家可用排笛驅鼠的遊戲機制。幽默感輕快，以接地氣口語翻譯。
